title: Jessie Buckley's Best Actress Oscar Win
text: Jessie Buckley won the Academy Award for Best Actress for her role in Hamnet, completing an unprecedented awards sweep. She became the first Irish actress to receive this honor. Buckley's performance as Agnes Shakespeare featured multiple challenging scenes including birth sequences, mourning her son's death, and a final 20-minute sequence at the Globe theater. She dominated the precursor awards season, winning the Golden Globe, Critics Choice, BAFTA, and Actor Awards before claiming the Oscar. In her remarks, Buckley expressed gratitude for the opportunity, stating she got to know this incandescent woman and journey to understand the capacity of a mother's love is the greatest collision of her life. She dedicated the award to mothers, noting it was Mother's Day in the U.K., and acknowledged a lineage of women who continue to thrive against all odds. Reviews praised her performance as ferocious and astounding, devastating, powerful, and sensational. Hamnet earned eight Oscar nominations total, including Best Picture and Best Director.
